Introducing a New Hypothesis in the Genesis of Breast Cancer: An Integrative Review

evidence_entities.text: Estrogen is currently identified as one of the main agents involved in the initiation of breast tumors
evidence indicates a greater risk when estrogen replacement is combined with progesterone, whereas its isolated use does not represent a significant risk
estrogen appears to increase the formation of the β and γ chains of Fg, as it suggests a potential link to the formation of Fg and its related proteins
FGL1 has been proposed to facilitate immune evasion by interacting with LAG-3 on tumor-infiltrating T cells
blocking the FGL1/LAG-3 interaction activates the T cell immune response in the tumor microenvironment
MCF-7 cells produce and secrete Fg polypeptides, mainly the intact γ chain and degraded Aα and Bβ chains
the isolated hormones promoted cell proliferation in a dose-dependent manner, and that the combination resulted in even greater proliferation
